edges.description: Failure of the somite-boundary / resegmentation program leaves adjacent cervical vertebrae fused.
intermediate_mechanisms: failure of sclerotome resegmentation between adjacent somites
edges.source: MEOX1 Somite and Sclerotome Polarity Defect

nodes.description: Fusion of two or more cervical vertebrae from failed segmentation.

evidence.snippet: Klippel-Feil syndrome (KFS) is a segmentation malformation of the cervical spine
they carry fusion defects and numerical abnormalities in the cervical spine, scoliosis, malformations of the cranial base, and Sprengel's deformity

nodes.description: Loss of MEOX1, a homeobox transcription factor with a nonredundant role in somite development, disrupts sclerotome polarity and the somite-boundary / resegmentation program in the paraxial mesoderm. KFS is the human phenotypic equivalent of the sclerotome polarity defect seen in Meox1-null mice. Autosomal dominant forms perturb the same axial-segmentation program via the BMP-family genes GDF6 and GDF3.
nodes.id: MEOX1 Somite and Sclerotome Polarity Defect

evidence.snippet: This gene encodes a transcription factor with a well-established and nonredundant role in somite development
KFS is the human phenotypic equivalent of the sclerotome polarity defect that results from Meox1 deficiency in mice.
